LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS STITCH FIX, INC. & KATRINA LAKE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS DENIED. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT THE AMENDMENT IS AMBIGUOUS AND THERE IS A CONFLICT IN THE PAROLE EVIDENCE REGARDING THE MEANING OF THE AMENDMENT IN LIGHT OF MS. LAKE'S EXPLANATION OF WHAT DOUBLE TRIGGER MEANT. THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION CONTENDING THAT PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION CONTRADICTS PARAGRAPH 8 OF PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, AND 10. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6 [FAILURE OF CONSIDERATION]. IT IS WELL ESTABLISHED THAT A CONSIDERATION FOR A CONTRACT IS EQUALLY VALUABLE WHETHER IT MOVE TO THE OTHER PARTY BOUND OR TO A THIRD PARTY. CONSIDERATION DOES NOT HAVE TO MOVE TO A PROMISOR." CECHETTINI V. CONSUMER ASSOC. LTD. (1968) 260 CAL.APP.2D 295, 298. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350 BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT SPECIFICALLY HIGHLIGHT THE ISSUES FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AND WHICH FACTS SUPPORT THOSE DISTINCT ISSUES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ERIN FLYNN'SMOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT COMPLIANCE WITH DISCOVERY REQUESTS IS DENIED. (CCP 2024.020(B). THE INITIAL TRIAL DATE WAS SET FOR NOVEMBER 12, 2013. ON JULY 23, 2013, THE COURT CONTINUED THE TRIAL DATE TO FEBRUARY 10, 2014 BASED UPON THE STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES. THE STIPULATION AND ORDER DOES NOT CONTEMPLATE AN EXTENSION OF DISCOVERY CUTOFF. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO MOVE TO REOPEN DISCOVERY PRIOR TO FILING HER INSTANT MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH DISCOVERY REQUESTS. (CCP 2024.050(A).) IT WOULD BE AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION FOR THE COURT TO SIMPLY HEAR THE MOTION COMPLE WITHOUT FIRST DECIDING WHETHER DISCOVERY SHOULD BE REOPENED. (PELTON-SHEPHERD INDUSTRIES, INC. V. DELTA PACKAGING PRODUCTS, INC. (2008) 165 CAL.APP.4TH 1568, 1588.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-15-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Erin Flynn'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Compliance With Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Erin Flynn'SMotion To Compel Defendant Compliance With Discovery Requests is denied. (CCP 2024.020(b). The initial trial date was set for November 12, 2013. On July 23, 2013, the Court continued the trial date to February 10, 2014 based upon the stipulation of the parties. The stipulation and order does not contemplate an extension of discovery cutoff. Plaintiff failed to move to reopen discovery prior to filing her instant motion to compel compliance with discovery requests. (CCP 2024.050(a).) It would be an abuse of discretion for the court to simply hear the motion comple without first deciding whether discovery should be reopened. (Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1568, 1588.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft Compliance Wtih Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF ERIN FLYNN'S Motion To Compel Defendant Compliance With Discovery Requests is transferred to be heard in department 302 at 9:30 a.m. on January 23, 2014. Judge Pro Tem Geoffrey Howard, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DAN QUALK, ESQ., AND ERIC KASTNER, ESQ., 650.967.7854, FOR PLAINTIFF ERIN MORRISON FLYNN.
JOSHUA BENSON, ESQ,. 415.788.8200, FOR DEFENDANTS STITCH FIX, INC. AND KATRINA LAKE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs STITCH FIX, INC. & KATRINA LAKE'S MOTION for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication as to cause of action 1 for breach of contract is denied. The parties agree that the amendment is ambiguous and there is a conflict in the parole evidence regarding the meaning of the amendment in light of Ms. Lake's explanation of what double trigger meant. The Court overrules defendant's objection contending that plaintiff's deposition contradicts paragraph 8 of plaintiff's declaration. Defendant fails to shift the burden as to causes of action 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, and 10. The motion is granted as to cause of action 6 [failure of consideration]. It is well established that a consideration for a contract is equally valuable whether it move to the other party bound or to a third party. Consideration does not have to move to a promisor." Cechettini v. Consumer Assoc. Ltd. (1968) 260 Cal.App.2d 295, 298. The Court further notes that defendant's separate statement of undisputed facts does not comply with CRC 3.1350 because defendant did not specifically highlight the issues for summary adjudication and which facts support those distinct issues. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
